purpose: Human/Codex disposition layer for BA findings. Raw BA reports stay unchanged; this file records investigated meaning and recommended action.

entries.reason: BA saw filter controls but nearby source shows combo boxes and checkbox connect to refresh handlers. Static scanner cannot prove visible runtime feedback, but no urgent app defect was confirmed.
entries.recommended_next_action: No app-code action now. Add a future PySide runtime/action-feedback adapter if Vellum feedback needs proof.

entries.reason: BA matched QPushButton inside generated QSS stylesheet text, not actual runtime button construction. This is scanner noise.
entries.recommended_next_action: Improve BA action scanner to ignore QSS/style template blocks; no Vellum app-code fix.

entries.reason: Collapsible section header button is wired to toggle body visibility and chevron state. BA cannot prove runtime perception from static scan, but behavior evidence is present.
entries.recommended_next_action: No immediate app-code action. Consider runtime click probe only if this UI becomes a release gate.

entries.reason: PASS/FAIL/SKIP buttons are checkable, exclusive, and emit result_changed. BA cannot prove visual styling/runtime feedback from static scan, but no defect was confirmed.
entries.recommended_next_action: No immediate app-code action. Runtime probe could verify checked-state visibility later.

entries.reason: Findings point at a docstring/comment and centralized color token fallbacks. This looks like rule locality overreach unless the Design Bible forbids central token definitions.
entries.recommended_next_action: No app-code action now. Improve lint rule locality/context handling or document token-definition allowance.
entries.machine_match_note: Updated after Vellum cleanup shifted BA lint row IDs; evidence paths remain the same.

entries.reason: BA/lint is seeing generated QSS placeholders such as ui_family and mono_family. Those resolve from design spec typography defaults; this is likely scanner context loss rather than a confirmed app defect.
entries.recommended_next_action: No app-code action now. Improve lint scanner to resolve or exempt generated stylesheet variables.
entries.machine_match_note: Updated after Vellum cleanup shifted BA lint row IDs; evidence paths remain the same.

evidence_reviewed: BA report unknown group: 12 PySide workflow order unproven rows
entries.reason: BA cannot prove PySide workflow order from current static markers. Unknown means coverage gap, not confirmed defect.
entries.recommended_next_action: Add PySide workflow metadata or runtime layout probe later if Vellum workflow order must be gated.

evidence_reviewed: BA report unknown groups: R19 info x18, R11 info x4, R09 info x1
entries.reason: Informational lint rows are not actionable defects without a specific violated rule and approved target behavior.
entries.recommended_next_action: Leave as coverage/context notes unless a future Vellum cleanup spec promotes specific rows.
entries.machine_match_note: Updated after Vellum cleanup shifted BA lint row IDs; this exact slash group matches current R09/R11/R19 info rows.

entries.reason: Each warning has click wiring and trace evidence of user-visible feedback through setNotice, render, panel visibility/loading, local handling state, button disabled/text updates, confirmation dialogs, or refreshed cockpit data. BA's static action-feedback scanner remains conservative for indirect feedback paths.
entries.recommended_next_action: No PAH app-code action. Treat as investigated scanner limitation; improve BA feedback tracing if these indirect UI feedback patterns need automatic proof.
entries.machine_match_note: Slash group matches the current PAH action-feedback warning IDs after BA scanner false-positive cleanup.

entries.reason: PAH correctly reports that the CC progress card is present but active_dispatch.json is absent, so CC activity can only be shown as idle. This is an operational/environment state, not a confirmed PAH app defect.
entries.recommended_next_action: No PAH app-code action. Have the CC sidecar writer create active_dispatch.json before treating CC activity as live-monitored.

entries.reason: PAH reports 14 open-on-agent threads and 0 owner-unknown threads. This is a real operational communication backlog to reconcile through protocol replies/archive state, not a PAH app-code bug.
entries.recommended_next_action: Operational follow-up: reconcile the backlog through PAH-visible replies or Codex/PAH reconciliation before code changes.
entries.machine_match_note: Exact PAH runtime warning for protocol.communication_backlog.

entries.reason: BA found 81 PAH HTML controls but no data-pg-order-group/data-pg-order markers, so it cannot statically prove Design Bible §1.7 workflow order. Investigation found no PAH-specific requirement that those markers already exist and no evidence of visible order breakage.
entries.recommended_next_action: No PAH app-code action now. Treat as coverage gap/scanner limitation unless PAH workflow-order proof becomes a release gate; then add explicit markers or a runtime geometry/order probe.
entries.machine_match_note: Exact PAH workflow-order unknown for missing HTML order markers.

resolved_entries.reason: 2px spacing is a real lint hit against the PG spacing scale, but it is low-risk visual polish rather than an urgent behavior bug.
resolved_entries.recommended_next_action: Include in a small future Vellum style cleanup spec.
resolved_entries.fix_summary: Vellum cleanup applied approved PG spacing/radius scale values; BA no longer emits these cleanup warnings.

resolved_entries.reason: 6px margins are real lint hits against the PG spacing scale, duplicated by the scanner. Low-risk style cleanup.
resolved_entries.recommended_next_action: Include in a small future Vellum style cleanup spec; dedupe repeated BA rows if possible.
resolved_entries.fix_summary: Vellum cleanup applied approved PG spacing/radius scale values; BA no longer emits these cleanup warnings.
